data.title: Intellia slips after mid-stage data for CRISPR-based hereditary angioedema therapy
data.description:
Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA) fell ~9% in the pre-market on Thursday after the biotech announced data from an ongoing Phase 2 trial for NTLA-2002, a CRISPR-based gene editing therapy for an inflammatory condition called hereditary angioedema (HAE).

Citing an Apr. 4 data cut based on 25 patients, Intellia (NASDAQ:NTLA) said 25 mg and 50 mg doses of NTLA-2002 reduced monthly HAE attack rates by 75% and 77% during weeks 1–16, and 80% and 81% during weeks 5–16, respectively.

Notably, eight of 11 patients in the 50 mg arm and four of the ten patients in the 25 mg arm witnessed a complete response after a single dose of NTLA-2002 compared to zero complete responses in the placebo arm.

Both dose levels were well tolerated, with headache and fatigue being among the most frequent adverse events among patients.

Based on the data, Intellia (NTLA) has selected the 50 mg dose for a Phase 3 pivotal study. The global trial identified as HAELO is currently enrolling patients.

data.title: Dave & Buster's gains as Eminence Capital said to pitch as long at Robin Hood conf
data.description:
Dave & Buster's Entertainment (NASDAQ:PLAY) rose 1.2% in premarket trading after Eminence Capital is said to have pitched the company as a long idea at the Robin Hood Investors Conference in New York City.

Eminence Capital's Ricky Sandler pitched Dave & Buster's (PLAY) at the charity conference on Wednesday, a person familiar with the presentation told Seeking Alpha.

Eminence Capital owned 3.8 million Dave & Buster's (PLAY) shares, or 9.6% stake, as of the end of June, making the investor one of the company's top five largest holders.

Dave & Buster's (PLAY) has short interest of 15%.

data.title: Harley-Davidson sales hit by high interest rates, soft consumer demand
data.description:
Harley-Davidson (NYSE:HOG) is set to trade in negative turf for a fifth consecutive day as the company’s latest quarterly results underscored soft demand for its high-margin motorcycles, leading to another downward revision to FY24 guidance.

“We have worked diligently through the quarter to mitigate the impact of high interest rates, and macroeconomic and political uncertainty, that continue to put pressure on our industry and customers, especially in our core markets," said CEO Jochen Zeitz, adding that the expectation for further interest rate reductions will provide the industry “with a needed tailwind.”

North American retail sales were down 10% year-over-year while global sales fell 13% resulting in a decline of 26% in consolidated revenue. Of the four types of bikes sold, sales of the Grand American touring took the biggest hit with deliveries down 64% year-over-year (revenue down 39%), followed by a 47% drop in Adventure Touring shipments, -43% for Cruiser, and -42% for Sport and Lightweight motorcycles.

Sales were also softest in Europe and the Middle East (-22%) followed by a 16% drop in Asia, 10% decline in the U.S., and -8% in Canada. Latin America was the only region where sales improved from a year ago (+4%).

The direction of sales resulted in a 34% decline in the company’s profits to $0.91 per share, 10 cents above what the Street expected. Gross margin narrowed 160 basis points to 30.1% while operating margin was sliced by more than half to 6.3%.

For FY24, Harley-Davidson (HOG) is forecasting a 14% to 16% decline in revenue, another downward revision from previous guidance for a decrease of 5% to 9%. Operating income is expected to increase 5%-10%, resulting in an operating income margin between 7.5% and 8.5% versus initial guidance of 10.6% to 11.6%. This compares to 2023 operating margin of 13.6%.

Shares are down 2.6%.

data.title: Marinus tanks after late stage trial for treatment of seizures in TSC fails to meet main goal
data.description:
• Marinus Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:MRNS) saw its shares drop about 65% premarket on Thursday after its late-stage trial of oral ganaxolone for treating seizures associated with tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC) in children and adults failed to meet the primary endpoint.
  • While reductions in seizure frequency favored the ganaxolone arm, the primary endpoint of percent change in 28-day TSC-associated seizure frequency, did not achieve statistical significance.
  • Ganaxolone was generally well-tolerated with a safety profile consistent with previous clinical trials.
  • As a result of the TrustTSC outcome, Marinus is discontinuing further ganaxolone clinical development and is taking additional steps to reduce costs, including a reduction in its workforce.
  • The company has also commenced a process to explore strategic alternatives with the goal of maximizing value for its stockholders.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sabre expands lodging content with agreement to add Premier Inn properties
data.description:
• Sabre Corporation (NASDAQ:SABR), on Thursday, announced a new distribution agreement with the UK’s hotel brand, Premier Inn, to ensure travel buyers worldwide have access to Premier Inn's expansive lodging content.
  • With over 900 properties and more than 85,000 rooms across the UK and Ireland, as well as 10,500 rooms in Germany, Premier Inn's properties are now available through Sabre's GDS.
  • This integration will enable travel agencies to book Premier Inn properties on the same platform as other travel components and services.
  • Sabre (SABR) shares were up more than 2% in pre-market trading on Thursday.

data.title: Allison Transmission announces expansion of its manufacturing facility in India
data.description:
Allison Transmission (NYSE:ALSN) announced on Thursday the expansion of its state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Chennai, India. The site is being used to support increasing global demand for Allison fully automatic transmissions. The company said the multi-year, over $100-million investment will double the existing manufacturing footprint of the Chennai facility.

In particular, the expansion will increase production capacity for Allison's (ALSN) on-highway fully automatic transmission portfolio, enhancing global operational flexibility and capability.

Allison Transmission (ALSN) said it continues to invest in its business to achieve its growth ambitions and deliver its brand promise to Improve the Way the World Works.

Shares of ALSN are up 8% over the last six weeks. The company is due to report earnings on October 29.

data.title: The Marygold Companies acquires 7.95% stake in Midland Capital
data.description:
• The Marygold Companies (NYSE:MGLD) said it has acquired a 7.95% ownership position, and invested $1.8 million, in Delaware based Midland Capital.
  • Midland Capital is a privately owned holding company.
  • As part of the deal, the firm also holds 180K warrants to purchase 180K additional shares of Midland Capital at an exercise price of $22.25.
  • The warrants are expected to expire on February 27, 2034.
